data.title: Akamai's Q2 results get largely neutral reactions at Piper Sandler
data.description:
Shares of Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) rose about 1% premarket on Friday after second quarter results beat estimates and the company raised its full year outlook, drawing largely neutral views at Piper Sandler.

Piper Sandler kept its Neutral rating on Akamai but lowered the price target on the shares to $83 from $88.

"Following a reset of shares over the last few weeks, shares are pointed slightly higher afterhours as Akamai was able to beat and raise for the year, though some of these gains were given back at the realization that the raise was primarily due to FX, TikTok inclusion, and CDN [Content Delivery Network] upside that offset Compute missing and Security coming inline," said analysts led by James Fish.

The analysts added that the company has many moving parts to it, even within sub-segments, and it will take some time to work through this transition. If successful, the transition could result in growth acceleration over the next few years back into at least over mid-single-digit territory, but if not, there is opportunity to take out costs to drive better profitability and free cash flow, or FCF.

"We see shares as range-bound still and reiterate our Neutral rating, with PT [price target] moving to $83 on lower FCF estimates," said Fish and his team.

Related stock: Cloudflare (NET) largely flat premarket on Friday.

data.title: LM Funding America’s bitcoin treasury valued $17.8M at July-end
data.description:
• LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA) mined 5.9 bitcoins last month, bringing its bitcoin holdings on July 31, 2025 to 150.4.
  • The company estimates that the value of its 150.4 bitcoin holdings was around $17.8 million or $3.461 per share, based on a bitcoin price of ~$118,100 as of July 31, 2025.
  • Bruce Rodgers, chairman and CEO of LM Funding, commented “In July, we reallocated a portion of our Bitcoin treasury to fund capacity expansion. July’s 7% increase in production and 20% growth in energy revenue reflect the reinvestment of treasury assets into operations—advancing our long-term Bitcoin accumulation strategy.”
  • LMFA +6.8% premarket.

data.title: Barrick to sell Chile gold project in $50M deal
data.description:
Barrick Mining (NYSE:B) said Friday it agreed to sell its Alturas gold project in Chile to a unit of Singapore’s Boroo for $50M in cash plus a 0.5% net smelter return royalty on gold and silver production from the project.

According to the deal terms, the royalty will end after 2M oz of gold and gold-equivalent are produced, and Boroo may buy it back within four years for $10M.

"This is another good example of an instance where an asset we own might be better suited in the hands of others while we pursue our priority portfolio of Barrick-managed growth projects," Barrick (NYSE:B) President and CEO Mark Bristow said.

Barrick (B) continues to own stakes in the Zaldivar copper mine, as well as the El Alto and Norte Abierto projects, in Chile.

data.title: Todd Tinelli to succeed Kevin Harrill as Centrus Energy CFO
data.description:
• Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) Friday announced the appointment of Todd Tinelli as Senior Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Treasurer, effective August 11. 
  • Tinelli replaces Kevin Harrill, who is resigning to pursue other opportunities after a four-year tenure as CFO and earlier as Chief Accounting Officer.
  • To ensure a seamless transition, Harrill will remain with the company through August 29.
  • Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) down 1.3% premarket.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: EXPE, MNST, GILD, BA
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, amid a rally in tech stocks that boosted market sentiment, even as investors continued to weigh the impact of Donald Trump's tariffs. Here are four stocks to watch for the day:  

Expedia (EXPE) soared 15% in premarket trade after the company raised its full-year outlook after its second-quarter results underscored consumers’ continued appetite for travel and exceeded Wall Street’s expectations. Total revenue increased 6.5% during the quarter to $3.79B, beating expectations by $80M.

Monster Beverage (MNST) rose ~8% before the opening bell after it beat its second-quarter profit estimates. The firm, however, missed its revenue consensus. Its Q2 adjusted EPS came in at $0.52, beating Wall Street estimates by $0.04.

Gilead Sciences (GILD) was up 4.5% in premarket trade as the company upped its 2025 sales and profit outlook, boosted by a growing demand for its HIV products. The firm also zoomed its Q2 revenue and profit estimates. The firm now expects its full-year adjusted profit to be in the range of $7.95 per share and $8.25 per share, as compared to its prior outlook of $7.70 per share and $8.10 per share.

Boeing (BA) shares will be keenly watched after the U.K. Competition and Markets Authority said it will not open an in-depth probe into Boeing's deal (BA) to acquire Spirit AeroSystems (SPR). This effectively cleared the $4.7B deal weeks after it launched the first phase of an investigation.

data.title: Universal Electronics expects connected home growth between 14% and 29% in Q3 2025 while closing Mexico facility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Universal Electronics Inc. (UEIC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Interim CEO Richard K. Carnifax emphasized the company's progress in capturing more of the home market with patented wireless control technology, highlighting a net cash position for the first time since December 2021. Carnifax noted, "In Q2, we delivered 46% revenue growth in connected home, helping to drive overall business gross margins to 29.9% and EPS of $0.18. Year-to-date operating cash flow was also very strong at almost $18 million."
  • Carnifax described ongoing scaling in core connected home accounts, including three new design projects with a major HVAC OEM for release in 2026 and 2027, as well as the shipment of a new smart home security product to a new customer in the DIY security and home automation space. The company is nearing completion on standard climate control solutions for delivery later this year in OEM, energy, and multi-dwelling unit channels.
  • Carnifax announced the decision to close the Mexico facility by year-end, citing strong productivity at Vietnam operations and lower volume in Mexico due to trends in home entertainment. He noted, "It is critical to customer continuity and stable financial performance that we have the flexibility to respond to business and regulatory changes, such as tariffs, that can quickly shift volumes and impact factories."
  • The company reported a positive development in its litigation against Roku, with Carnifax stating, "The court has ruled in our favor to consolidate our actions and move forward with the case... this latest ruling is a very positive sign that UEI will finally be able to move forward and present our case in court."
  • CFO Bryan M. Hackworth reported, "In the second quarter of 2025, net sales grew 8% to $97.7 million compared to $90.5 million for the second quarter of 2024. Sales in our connected home channel continued to exhibit strong growth as sales increased by $10.8 million or 46% to $34.1 million for the quarter ending June 30, 2025."

Outlook

  • For Q3 2025, Hackworth stated, "We expect sales to range from $92 million to $102 million compared to $102.1 million in the third quarter of 2024. We expect connected home sales to range from $30 million to $34 million compared to $26.4 million in the third quarter of 2024, representing growth between 14% and 29%. In home entertainment, we expect sales to range from $62 million to $68 million compared to $75.7 million in the third quarter of 2024, representing a 10% to 18% decline. We expect EPS to range from $0.08 to $0.18 per diluted share compared to $0.10 per diluted share in the third quarter of 2024."
  • The company expects total revenue to decline in Q3 and Q4 year over year, citing inconsistency in connected home orders and continued pressure in home entertainment. For full year 2026, management expects "new and existing connected home product projections to support growth in the channel."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q2 2025 were $97.7 million. Connected home sales were $34.1 million, up $10.8 million or 46%. Home entertainment sales were $63.6 million, down $3.6 million or 5%.
  • Gross profit was $29.2 million, or 29.9% of sales, up from 28.3% in the first quarter of 2025 and 28.7% in Q2 2024. Hackworth attributed improvement to operational gains in Vietnam and favorable currency fluctuations.
  • Operating expenses decreased to $26.3 million from $27.1 million in Q2 2024. SG&A expenses were $19.5 million, while R&D expenses were $6.8 million for Q2 2025.
  • Operating income was $2.9 million versus a loss of $1.1 million last year. Net income was $2.4 million compared to a net loss of $1.2 million a year ago.
  • As of June 30, 2025, net cash position was $4.1 million, with $34.3 million in cash and $30.2 million in debt. Operating cash flow year-to-date was $17.7 million.

Q&A

  • Steven Bruce Frankel, Rosenblatt Securities: Asked about North American trends and customer concentration. Hackworth responded, "We see the trends continuing in line with what we've seen so far. I think the problem is they're being offset by what we covered in the call relative to Latin America and Europe." Hackworth noted Daikin at 18.7% and Comcast at 12.2% for customer concentration.
  • Frankel probed for Q4 historical figures and guidance. Hackworth clarified, "We're not providing specific guidance. All we're saying -- we're just giving general guidance from a top level saying that right now, as we currently stand in the fourth quarter, I expect the sales in total to be down sequentially, actually for both them as well, both connected home and home entertainment."
  • Frankel inquired about tariff impacts and supply chain adjustments. Carnifax said, "We're confident we can manage through that and are working closely with customers and suppliers on managing that. And that's reflected in our margin guidance for the rest of the year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral, with questions focused on customer concentration, geographic trends, sequential sales outlook, and tariff management. There was a slight increase in concern regarding revenue variability and exposure to tariffs, but no overt negativity.
  • Management maintained a confident and pragmatic tone, emphasizing operational improvements and strategic flexibility. Carnifax stated, "We are confident in our ability to continue this in the future, as we have built this into our supply chain through both internal capacity and external partners."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management exhibited increased confidence, especially regarding cash position and gross margin, while analysts' tone remained consistent but slightly more focused on risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious optimism in Q1 to acknowledgment of near-term revenue declines in Q3 and Q4, with long-term optimism for connected home growth in 2026.
  • The strategic focus sharpened on connected home, with explicit mention of new design projects and customer launches, while the decision to close the Mexico facility signals a significant operational shift.
  • Key metrics showed connected home growth accelerating, gross margin improving, and a shift to positive operating and net income. Analysts' focus moved toward sequential sales risks and tariff exposure.
  • Management's confidence increased, particularly regarding cost control, profitability, and ability to navigate regulatory and market challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the inconsistency in connected home order patterns, noting this could impact quarterly revenue forecasts in the latter half of 2025.
  • Continued decline is expected in the home entertainment business segment, especially in EMEA and Latin America.
  • Tariffs and regulatory changes remain a business risk, but management indicated preparedness, citing supply chain flexibility and prior experience.
  • Litigation risk persists, though the recent court ruling in favor of UEI was described as a positive development.

Final Takeaway

Universal Electronics delivered strong results in connected home, improved margins, and achieved a net cash position, while announcing the closure of its Mexico facility to drive operational efficiency. Management projects continued growth in connected home for 2026 despite acknowledging short-term revenue headwinds and volatility. Strategic initiatives are aimed at sustaining profitability, scaling new product launches, and maintaining supply chain flexibility in response to regulatory changes and market demand.
